title: Pivot identitaire
video_url: Highly specific search: 'Strategic pivot in decentralized autonomous organizations and community governance'
sources: README.md: Project philosophy and phase evolution
quests.ts: Foundational Quest Specifications for District A
forom-config.ts: Default category and question label mappings
database.sql: Memo and user table schemas for local PostgreSQL
App.tsx: Pixel reward logic and role-based context
resume: The quest "Pivot identitaire" (A9) is positioned at the intersection of the Vision district and the Urgence action within the 10x10 Finite Rom Map grid[cite: 2, 3]. In the Forom ecosystem, the "Urgence" tag (9) is reserved for megathreads or critical shifts that demand immediate attention from the entire community[cite: 1, 3]. As part of Phase 1: The Foundations (V1), this quest addresses a total redirection of the community's identity or core mission, such as "Saving human communities & long-term memory"[cite: 1]. This occurs when the foundational philosophy established in A0-A8 is no longer viable and requires a strategic pivot. Technically, this state is recorded in the local PostgreSQL "memos" table, with the "question" field mapped to '9' and the "category_id" linking it to Vision. The React 19 and Vite 7 frontend renders this critical update within the spatial 5x5 viewport, ensuring that every member is immediately aware of the shift[cite: 1]. During such a pivot, the Guardian role is essential for maintaining stability and guiding new users through the transition[cite: 1]. Guardians, aligned with the color red, enforce rules to prevent chaos during identity shifts[cite: 1]. The Pixel (PX) economy facilitates this transition; in Phase 1, completing a foundational quest like A9 rewards the contributor with 2 PX, redistributing purchasing power as the node evolves[cite: 1]. This is vital because the server's long-term success depends on a cohesive archive of 100 high-quality memos[cite: 1]. If an identity pivot is required, it must be documented clearly so the future local AI, ROM, is trained on the most accurate and up-to-date version of the community lore[cite: 1]. By hosting this data locally on an NVIDIA Jetson, the community ensures that even major strategic shifts remain private and sovereign, protected from external interference[cite: 1]. This archived memory is a key part of the V1 Archive, set to be refined in Phase 2 before the Phase 3 "Awakening" ritual[cite: 1]. This documentation ensures knowledge transfer for future generations, like the 70 ETS Student Clubs in the pilot project, by providing a historical record of why and how the community adapted its vision[cite: 1]. A9 ensures that the forom's mission remains resilient and adaptable over a 10-year span[cite: 1]. This pivot is the final safeguard against stagnation, allowing the community to redefine its core values while maintaining the structural integrity of its finite grid[cite: 1]. Every entry in the A9 slot becomes a permanent record of the community's evolution and survival strategy. Ultimately, this quest protects the mission by ensuring that the architectural framework stays aligned with human reality. This ensures that the global public lobby receives a coherent community identity once connectivity is earned through rigorous phase progression.
summary: This quest facilitates a critical redirection of the community mission at coordinate A9, ensuring that strategic identity shifts are archived as sovereign deep lore.
